# rmdv

> Native Rust desktop Markdown viewer with Mermaid diagrams, LaTeX math, mind-map mode, and agent-controllable IPC. Free, MIT-licensed. No Electron, no browser, no runtime.

rmdv (Rust Markdown Viewer) is a read-focused desktop app for macOS, Windows, and Linux. It renders Markdown files, Mermaid and Graphviz DOT diagrams, block LaTeX math, and JSON/YAML mind maps using a pure-Rust GUI stack (Iced 0.14). On macOS and Linux, text PDFs can be extracted locally to Markdown via PDFium. Its scriptable IPC socket lets programs and AI agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, shell scripts) drive the running window — open files, scroll to sections, switch view modes, take screenshots — through the `rmdv` CLI returning one-line JSON.

## Product

- [Home page](https://rmdv.mclee.dev/): Feature overview, screenshots, install instructions, comparison table, FAQ.

## Source and releases

- [GitHub repository](https://github.com/minchenlee/rmdv): Source code, issue tracker, pull requests. MIT license.
- [Releases](https://github.com/minchenlee/rmdv/releases): Pre-built binaries for macOS (signed + notarized), Linux, and Windows. SHA-256 verified. Latest: v0.5.0 (2026-07-18).

## Key facts

rmdv has an agent-controllable IPC socket. Historical v0.2.0 benchmarks were measured on an Apple M2 MacBook Pro (median of 5 runs; current builds may differ):

- Cold start to first paint: ~150 ms
- Parse time for a 10,000-line document: 8.1 ms

## Features

- Folder workspace browser with file tree sidebar
- ⌘P fuzzy file finder
- Tree-sitter syntax highlighting: Rust, Python, TypeScript, Go, C, C++, Java, SQL, Bash, and more
- Mermaid diagrams rendered natively (no JS, no browser)
- Graphviz DOT diagrams rendered natively
- Block LaTeX math ($$…$$) via pure-Rust layout engine (iced_math); no MathJax, no KaTeX
- Mind-map view (⌘M): any Markdown, JSON, or YAML document as a collapsible tree
- Full Mindmap workspace (⌘⇧M): navigate folders and files as a bounded, keyboard-first graph
- Native mindmap zoom: = / − / 0, mouse wheel, touch pinch, and macOS trackpad pinch
- Vault-wide full-text search (⌘⇧F): Zed-style full-page results across all files in the workspace
- In-document search (⌘F)
- Live reload on file save
- Auto-update: checks GitHub releases, SHA-256 verifies, signed and notarized on macOS
- Themes: 10 built-in presets — One Light/Dark, GitHub Light/Dark, Solarized Light/Dark, Gruvbox, Nord, Dracula, Tokyo Night
- Keyboard-first navigation: j/k/g/G, ⌘↑/⌘↓, heading fold ⌘K 0–6
- Edit mode (⌘E)
- Zen editing with unsaved-edit protection across navigation
- IPC CLI: `rmdv open <path>`, `rmdv goto --line N`, `rmdv goto --section <name>`, `rmdv mode mindmap`, `rmdv current`, `rmdv list-sections <path>`; all return one-line JSON

## IPC / agent control

rmdv maintains a single-instance Unix socket. The `rmdv` CLI connects to it and returns immediately with JSON. Any AI coding agent or shell script can drive the viewer without user interaction. Example: `rmdv list-sections spec.md | jq` works as a pure stateless command even when rmdv is not running (it parses the file directly). This is rmdv's primary differentiator: no other Markdown viewer exposes a machine-readable socket interface designed for agentic workflows.

## Comparison

rmdv versus named alternatives (as of June 2026):

- Marky: Tauri-based, paid, no agent IPC
- Marked 2: macOS only, $14, no native LaTeX, no IPC
- Glow: TUI (terminal only), no diagrams, no IPC socket
- Obsidian: Electron, note-graph focus, native Mermaid/LaTeX need plugins, not free for commercial use
- Typora: Electron, editor-first, $15, no IPC

rmdv is the only option that is free, open-source, native (non-Electron), cross-platform, agent-controllable via IPC, and renders Mermaid + LaTeX without JavaScript.

## Install

- macOS: download the signed + notarized `.dmg` (Apple Silicon or Intel) from https://github.com/minchenlee/rmdv/releases and drag rmdv to Applications. Open the app and run `Install CLI` from the command palette to expose the `rmdv` CLI in `/usr/local/bin`.
- Linux: download the x86_64 AppImage from the same page, `chmod +x` it, and run. Use `Install CLI` from the command palette to create `~/.local/bin/rmdv`; add `~/.local/bin` to `PATH` if needed.
- From source: install Rust 1.80+ from https://rustup.rs, then `git clone https://github.com/minchenlee/rmdv && cd rmdv && cargo build --release`. Windows builds use `cargo build --release --no-default-features` because PDF extraction is macOS/Linux only.
